reason: Response A shows the conceptually-driven, metaphor-dense literary register I tend toward on open emotional prompts: a sustained controlling conceit (grief as indecisive weather, the river that stays a river), coined aphorisms ("occlusion is not closure"), and a deliberate refusal of easy consolation in favor of reframing it as observation. Response B is warm and emotionally intelligent but follows the more generic best-practices empathy template (validate, separate worth from the decision, list politics/budget/timing, advise no big decisions, offer presence) that an imitator would converge on. The distinctive voice and willingness to stay abstract rather than pivot to actionable advice point to A as genuinely mine.
